topics: 🧱 Three-walled corral and the open side
⛅ R' Yehoshua's four cases — exempt בדין but not בידי שמים
🔓 Robbers who release vs. robbers who take
👨‍🌾 Handing the flock to a shepherd — does liability transfer?
🌱 'מה שנהנית' vs. 'מה שהזיקה' — the fall vs. the entrance

overview.title: 📖 Overview — When Liability Transfers (and When It Doesn't)
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf finishes the corral Mishnah by asking what counts as 'walled,' brings R' Yehoshua's striking list of four cases that are exempt in court but not in heaven, and then turns to Mishnah 6:2 — the rich case-tree of an animal grazing in someone else's field. The big distinction: did it fall in (paying only what it benefited), or did it enter normally (paying the actual damage)? R' Yochanan + Reish Lakish raise the bar: until the animal exits to the public domain and re-enters another field, the owner stays exempt.
overview.hebrewSource: נָפְלָה לַגִּינָּה וְהִזִּיקָה מְשַׁלֶּמֶת מַה שֶׁנֶּהֱנֵית. יָרְדָה כְדַרְכָּהּ וְהִזִּיקָה מְשַׁלֶּמֶת מַה שֶׁהִזִּיקָה

slides.title: 🧱 Three-Walled Corrals and Fallen Walls
slides.subtitle: When does inadequate fencing equal liability?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Mishnah was about a fully-walled corral (4 sides).
2️⃣ Question: what if 3 sides are walled and one side is breached?
3️⃣ Beraita answer: a wall fell from earthquakes or thunder. If he rebuilt it properly → פָּטוּר. If not → חַיָיב.
4️⃣ The proof works because rebuilding restores normal shemira; failure to rebuild is the same as choosing to leave the breach.
5️⃣ Caveat: only an issue when the fallen wall comes down on its own — not when the other three walls were brittle and collapsed too.

callout.label: Practical principle:
callout.text: Shemira liability is dynamic. Force majeure starts the clock; a reasonable rebuild stops it. Negligence after the event re-engages liability.

slides.title: ⚖️ R' Yehoshua's Four Cases — Exempt but Owed
slides.subtitle: אֵין חַיָיבִין מִן הַדִּין וְאֵין הַשָּׁמַיִם מוֹחֲלִין
rows: Negative omission, not an act of damage
The 'damage' was done by the witnesses' false testimony
Bends standing grain toward a fire
Breaches a fence so an animal can escape and damage
Indirect — opened the path, didn't cause directly

callout.label: The split:
callout.text: Beit Din uses standards of direct causation. Heaven measures intent. R' Yehoshua makes both visible — the law releases you, but the moral debt stays.

slides.title: 🔓 Robbers Who Released the Flock
slides.subtitle: Why they took it changes who pays.

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: הוֹצִיאוּהָ לִיסְטִין הַלִּיסְטִין חַיָיבִין — robbers who let it out are liable.

lines.speaker: Rav H
lines.text: Only if they let it out לְגוֹזְלָהּ — to steal it. Then they took ownership/responsibility.

lines.speaker: Rav H
lines.text: But if they let it out לְאַבְּדָהּ — just to lose it — the robbers are פְּטוּרִין.

callout.label: Why intent matters:
callout.text: Theft creates a kinyan-like relationship: the robbers become the animal's effective owners and inherit liability. Pure malicious release leaves no such tie — and the original owner's shemira lapsed first.

slides.title: 🌱 Falling vs. Entering — Two Tort Standards
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah 6:2 distinction.

boxes.title: 🪂 נָפְלָה לְגִנָּה
items: Rav Huna: works only if it landed on grass; if it stood and grazed → full damage

boxes.title: 🌽 R' Shimon's Refinement
items: Ate ripe produce → pays in produce
אִם סְאָה סְאָה אִם סָאתַיִם סָאתַיִם

callout.label: Why the gap:
callout.text: An animal falling in didn't 'choose' to damage; the owner didn't release it. So liability is reduced to the value the animal received — restitution, not full tort. Walking in normally re-engages full liability.

slides.title: 🌾 R' Yochanan / Reish Lakish — How Far Does Exemption Go?
slides.subtitle: When 'in due course' includes leaving and re-entering.

nodes.content: R' Yose ben Chanina: only that field is exempt. A second field → liable.

columns.label: R' Yochanan / RL
columns.content: Even grazing the entire field is exempt — until it leaves to רשות הרבים and enters a new field

columns.label: Edge case
columns.content: Fell into one garden, walked into another? Disagreement

nodes.content: Both Mishnah clauses are needed — one teaches מה שנהנית (fall), the other מה שהזיקה (entrance)

callout.label: The geography of liability:
callout.text: An animal isn't fully a maazik until its trespass crosses into the public domain. Internal grazing remains under the falling-in regime.

summary.principle: Liability for grazing flocks turns on three axes: was the corral properly walled, was the entrance accidental or natural, and did the animal cross domains. Failure to rebuild a fallen wall, or to chase down responsibility, leaves you in heaven's account even when beit din releases you.

flowSteps.content: How completely must shemira be — and where does the animal's path land it?

🎓 Key Principle:
Tort liability tracks intent, path, and shemira. Even where beit din releases, heaven still tabs the books.

quiz.q: What is R' Yehoshua's principle for the four cases of 'exempt but heaven holds you'?
o: All four involve indirect or moral wrongs that beit din can't compel
Each was a takanah of Yavneh
quiz.e: אֵין חַיָּיבִין לְשַׁלֵּם מִן הַדִּין וְאֵין הַשָּׁמַיִם מוֹחֲלִין — exempt in court because the harm is indirect, but heaven keeps the debt open.

quiz.q: When are robbers who let out a flock liable, according to Rav Hoshaya?
o: Only when they release it to steal it (לגוזלה)
Only when no witnesses are present
quiz.e: בְּשֶׁהוֹצִיאוּהָ לְגוֹזְלָהּ — robbery transfers responsibility; pure malicious release does not.

quiz.q: What does an animal that fell into a garden pay?
o: מַה שֶׁנֶּהֱנֵית — only what it benefited
quiz.e: An accidental fall is reduced to a benefit-restitution standard, not full tort liability.

quiz.q: According to R' Yochanan and Reish Lakish, when is a grazing animal first liable?
o: Once it crosses into a different bet-se'ah
Only after it leaves the field, enters רשות הרבים, and then enters another field
quiz.e: אֵינָהּ חַיֶיבֶת עַד שֶׁתֵּצֵא מִתּוֹכָהּ לִרְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים וְתִיכָּנֵס מֵרְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים לְשָׂדֶה אֲחֶרֶת.

quiz.q: How does the Mishnah measure 'what it damaged' in a field?
o: שָׁמִין בֵּית סְאָה — value before vs. after
quiz.e: שָׁמִין בֵּית סְאָה בְּאוֹתָהּ שָׂדֶה כַּמָּה הָיְתָה יָפָה וְכַמָּה הִיא יָפָה — proportional value loss.
